A lock-free ring buffer manages wraparound by treating the underlying array as a circular space where indices are incremented modulo the buffer size, ensuring that producers and consumers seamlessly transition from the end of the memory block back to the beginning without explicit boundary checks or conditional branching. Memory ordering is critical to prevent race conditions, requiring atomic operations with specific acquire and release semantics so that a producer’s write to a data slot is visible to the consumer only after the index update is committed, thereby avoiding scenarios where a consumer reads stale or partially written data. Backpressure is typically implemented by having the producer check if the buffer is full before attempting to write, often returning
